topics.headline: AB 126 just changed the rules for nonclassroom-based charter schools — effective immediately
topics.why_matters: Nearly every Tier A partner (Visions, Sky Mountain, South Sutter, Ocean Grove, Heartwood, iLEAD Online/Hybrid, Sage Oak, Compass, Pacific Coast Academy, Heartland, etc.) is an NCB charter. AB 126 requires them to itemize contract costs related to staff and instruction — meaning A+ invoices and service agreements are about to be scrutinized line-by-line inside their funding determinations. Simultaneously, the new $1B ongoing community schools stream explicitly excludes them. A+ is one of the few vendors that can honestly say: our reporting stack was already built to itemize by student, by minute, by skill.
topics.angle: "AB 126 just made your funding determination harder. Here's what NCB charter directors are asking us about this week." Frame A+ as the vendor whose invoicing and outcome documentation already meets the new itemization bar.
topics.roman_take: The conviction cut — Sacramento keeps writing rules that treat NCB charters as second-class LEAs. If the state is going to demand line-item accountability, then partners should demand line-item accountability from every vendor they hire. That's the standard A+ has always operated at.
topics.danielle_take: The implementation cut — a walk-through of exactly which line items on a typical A+ invoice map to the new itemization requirement, and what a partner director should ask any tutoring vendor to hand over before September funding determinations. ---

topics.headline: iLEAD Flex opens in Lancaster — Tier A partner expansion post-NCB moratorium
topics.category: C (Tier A partner news) — primary; A (state policy) — secondary
topics.why_matters: iLEAD is one of A+'s largest active partners across the Southern CA cluster. iLEAD Flex is expected to open with approximately 750 learners, bringing iLEAD's total network enrollment to nearly 7,000 students. The launch is part of a longer growth plan that includes iLEAD Innovate, the network's next planned campus, which would be their first school outside of Los Angeles County and is expected to open in fall 2027. Since the statewide moratorium on new nonclassroom-based charters concluded on January 1, 2026, mission-driven networks like iLEAD have been moving quickly to bring their programs to communities that have long lacked access — meaning more iLEAD-shaped opportunities are coming, fast. Every new campus needs an intervention layer on day one.
topics.angle: Congratulate iLEAD on the Lancaster launch; frame the NCB-moratorium-lift moment as a wave of new charters that will need intervention infrastructure built in, not bolted on.
topics.roman_take: New charters can't afford to spend Year 1 discovering they have an LTEL problem in Year 3 — bake in Tier 2/3 intervention capacity before you open the doors.
topics.danielle_take: Practical checklist for a launching charter: how to design intervention delivery around classroom-based instruction, Independent Study, or hybrid options from the first bell so Title I/Title III dollars land on structured services, not scramble.

topics.headline: Stanford NSSA RCT . "Having access to an AI tutor isn't the same as using it"
topics.why_matters: Stanford SCALE's Carly Robinson stated that "having access to this AI tutor isn't the same as using it" — researchers found that when two school districts carved out dedicated time for hundreds of elementary students to work with a well-known AI reading tutor, students didn't use it much at all. The RCTs tested elementary students assigned to use an AI literacy platform independently or with an in-person tutor focused on engagement. Every competitor in A+'s space — Varsity Tutors, IXL, Carnegie Learning, and every AI-native entrant — is currently pitching AI tutoring as the answer to district budget pressure. This is the first Tier-1 research result publicly poking holes in that pitch. A+ can say something *only A+ can say*: our model is engagement-first, humans-first, and our attendance/completion data proves it. Nobody with an AI product can credibly counter this.
topics.angle: "Stanford just published what we've been saying for 20 years. Access isn't intervention. Attendance is."
topics.roman_take: Call out the venture-backed AI-tutoring pitch deck directly — the industry has spent $12B convincing districts that scale = software, and Stanford just handed us the RCT proving scale without engagement equals nothing.
topics.danielle_take: Walk charter directors through what "engagement-designed" tutoring actually looks like at implementation — tutor consistency, small groups, session cadence, the specific things NSSA's own standards require that a login screen can't provide.
